sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Patient reports high blood pressure and headaches.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports struggling with high blood pressure for the last few months, stating it's often above 160/95. He also reports experiencing headaches, which he describes as dull and persistent, occurring several times a week. The patient's past medical history includes a kidney transplant performed approximately five years ago and current immunosuppression medications. He is currently taking norvasc. The patient reports feeling generally well, with no other significant symptoms. He denies any recent trauma or changes in his medication regimen.
Findings
Blood pressure is elevated at 158/92. The patient reports headaches. Physical exam reveals no acute findings. Complete metabolic panel is pending. Lipid panel is pending.
Assessment
The patient's primary diagnoses include high blood pressure and headaches. He also has a history of kidney transplant and is currently taking immunosuppression medications.
Plan
Order a complete metabolic panel and a lipid panel. Prescribe carvedilol 25 milligrams twice a day. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess response to medication and review lab results. Advise the patient to monitor his blood pressure at home and report any significant changes.
